inline_comments.message: I think it is better to remove lines 153-160.   I don't think it will add junit coverage, and it is junitted quite a bit in policy-endpoints.   My concern is because the sleep() interval may be sufficient in some cases, others not, depending on the jenkins server load, so may cause some junit failures once in a while.   Do you know if the jenkins failures you got before are related to this?   I can access older logs (have been deleted).
I had looked at the logs for the previous build failures. The first was due to my code, the subsequent failures all failed in the policy-endpoints module.
I missed this, in the else case, you may want to add a fail statement

A fail here wouldn't be appropriate. By default, each Reports object contains a Report on the status of the PDP-D. Which in this junit is not alive because we don't need to test it. When we iterate onto the Report object for the PDP-D we just want to ignore it, not fail the junit.

This is what the Reports string looks like during the junit:
Reports [healthy=false, details=[
  Report 
   [name=PDP-D, url=self, healthy=false,
    code=500, message=not alive],
  Report 
   [name=HEALTHCHECK, 
    url=http://localhost:7777/healthcheck/test,
    healthy=true, code=200, message=All Alive]
   ]
  ]
